SEARCH
Package com.berryrock.integrationhub.client

Class BuildiumClientImpl

java.lang.Object
com.berryrock.integrationhub.client.BuildiumClientImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
BuildiumClient
@Component public class BuildiumClientImpl extends Object implements BuildiumClient
REST client implementation for the Buildium property management API. Part of the client layer — fetches rental unit and active-lease records from the Buildium v1 REST API using a pre-configured WebClient. Pagination is handled internally via an offset loop that continues until a page smaller than PAGE_SIZE is returned. Address records are built by joining unit data (which carries the physical address) with active lease data (which carries the lease ID) on the shared unit ID. Units that have no matching active lease are still returned with a null lease ID. When integration.vendor.buildium.enabled is false, all methods return empty results without making any HTTP calls.

  • Constructor Details

    • BuildiumClientImpl

      public BuildiumClientImpl(org.springframework.web.reactive.function.client.WebClient buildiumWebClient, BuildiumProperties properties)
      Constructs the client with the Buildium-specific web client and configuration properties.
      Parameters:
      buildiumWebClient - a pre-configured WebClient with the Buildium base URL and credential headers already set
      properties - Buildium connection settings, including the enabled flag

  • Method Details

    • ping

      public boolean ping()
      Description copied from interface: BuildiumClient
      Performs a lightweight connectivity check against the Buildium API. Attempts to fetch a single page of rentals with a limit of 1. A non-null response is considered a successful ping. Returns false immediately if the integration is disabled.
      Specified by:
      ping in interface BuildiumClient
      Returns:
      true if the Buildium API responded successfully; false otherwise

    • getRentalsPage

      public List<Map<String,Object>> getRentalsPage(int limit, int offset)
      Description copied from interface: BuildiumClient
      Fetches a single page of rental properties from the Buildium /v1/rentals endpoint.
      Specified by:
      getRentalsPage in interface BuildiumClient
      Parameters:
      limit - maximum number of records to return in this page
      offset - zero-based record offset for pagination
      Returns:
      list of raw property maps as returned by the API; empty list on error or no data

    • getAllRentals

      public List<Map<String,Object>> getAllRentals()
      Description copied from interface: BuildiumClient
      Fetches all rental properties by repeatedly calling BuildiumClient.getRentalsPage(int, int) until the API returns a partial page.
      Specified by:
      getAllRentals in interface BuildiumClient
      Returns:
      complete list of raw property maps; empty list if the integration is disabled or an error occurs

    • fetchActiveLeaseAddresses

      public List<BuildiumAddressRecord> fetchActiveLeaseAddresses()
      Description copied from interface: BuildiumClient
      Fetches all active-lease address records by joining the units and leases endpoints. Performs three internal API calls:
      1. Fetches all rental units (which carry the address data)
      2. Fetches all active leases (which carry the unit-to-lease mapping)
      3. Joins the two result sets on unit ID to produce typed BuildiumAddressRecord objects
      Units without a street address are skipped. Units with no associated active lease still produce a record but with buildiumLeaseId set to null.
      Specified by:
      fetchActiveLeaseAddresses in interface BuildiumClient
      Returns:
      list of address records for all units with a street address; empty list if the integration is disabled or an error occurs
